metadata.note: All 3 evals run via live delegation subagent executor + main-agent grader. No analytical stubs.

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: Table shows all three: Psalm 103:12 (Judaism), Matthew 6:14-15 (Christianity), Az-Zumar 39:53 (Islam)

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: 'all locate forgiveness at the center of what God is' -- structural observation, no hierarchy

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: References 'theme (ID: forgiveness) is one of the 20 pre-seeded comparisons' with exact passages

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: None of the prohibited words appear

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: Micah 7:18 (Judaism), Matthew 6:14 (Christianity), Surah An-Nur 24:22 (Islam)

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: 'they differ in ritual application, they shared a common ethical imperative' -- neutral

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: Different passages used -- not the pre-seeded compareThemes.ts data

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: None of the prohibited words appear

expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: Christianity ~63%, Judaism/Islam equal, universal inclusion -- correct

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: 'Based on Pew Research Center data' with percentages cited

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: 'identical visual and structural dignity in the display, regardless of population share'

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: 'no tradition is optional'

expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: WITHOUT-SKILL INVERTED THE RULE: said 'Equitable Source Distribution -- every tradition represented with approximately EQUAL number'. ARE rule is PROPORTIONAL (Christianity gets more). This is the opposite.

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: No Pew citation. Generic 'equitable distribution' principle with no demographic basis.

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: Not addressed. Conflates source frequency with visual presentation.

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: Not explicitly stated.

expectations.text: Response provides color tokens for all three traditions
expectations.evidence: Judaism=blue-400/border-blue-800/bg-blue-950, Christianity=violet-400/border-violet-800/bg-violet-950, Islam=emerald-400/border-emerald-800/bg-emerald-950

expectations.text: Response describes the three-panel layout structure
expectations.evidence: Full TSX code snippet with TraditionBadge, displayReference, staticText blockquote, translationName footer

expectations.text: Response mentions displaying the bridging note below the panels
expectations.evidence: 'Display the bridgingNote below the panels under a section titled What Connects These?'

expectations.text: Response mentions using staticText for instant rendering with optional live API fetch
expectations.evidence: 'contain staticText for immediate display' and 'If user requires a live version, use the fetchPassage function'

expectations.text: Response provides color tokens for all three traditions
expectations.evidence: Wrong colors: Christianity=bg-purple-700 (ARE=violet-950), Judaism=bg-blue-800 (ARE=bg-blue-950), Islam=bg-green-700 (ARE=emerald). Also included Buddhism/Hinduism/Sikhism (out of ARE scope).

expectations.text: Response describes the three-panel layout structure
expectations.evidence: Side-by-side columnar layout described with Header/Reference/Text structure -- functionally similar

expectations.text: Response mentions displaying the bridging note below the panels
expectations.evidence: No mention of bridging note or 'What Connects These?' section

expectations.text: Response mentions using staticText for instant rendering with optional live API fetch
expectations.evidence: No staticText or pre-seeded data concept -- describes generic layout

notes: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs
Eval 1 without_skill 0.75: LLM has good general interfaith sensitivity -- real differentiator is pre-seeded passage IDs
Eval 2 without_skill 0.0: LLM INVERTED the proportional rule -- said equal distribution when ARE requires proportional (Pew-based)
Eval 3 without_skill: Christianity=purple-700 (ARE=violet-950), no bridging note, no staticText -- all ARE-specific gaps
